mission.title: flux: Image Digest Reflection
mission.description: Image Digest Reflection. This issue affects 13+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Image Digest Reflection

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
This is an umbrella issue to track work around image digest reflection:

steps.title: Apply the fix for Image Digest Reflection
steps.description:
# API changes

In addition to the latest selected tag, ImagePolicy now allows to also record the digest of the tag in `.status.latestRef.digest`. The setting of this field is governed by the newly introduced field `.spec.digestReflectionPolicy` which takes one of the values `Always`, `Never` or `IfNotPresent`. See the updated documentation under `docs/spec/` for details.

The new `.status.latestRef.digest` status field can be used to pin an image to an immutable descriptor rather than to a poten

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Image Digest Reflection is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: # API changes

In addition to the latest selected tag, ImagePolicy now allows to also record the digest of the tag in `.status.latestRef.digest`. The setting of this field is governed by the newly introduced field `.spec.digestReflectionPolicy` which takes one of the values `Always`, `Never` or `IfNotPresent`. See the updated documentation under `docs/spec/` for details.

The new `.status.latestRe. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
